title: abutilon hirtum
intro: Abutilon hirtum, also known as the hairy velvet flower or Indian mallow, is a plant species in the family Malvaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Abutilon hirtum include treating skin conditions such as wounds, eczema, and dermatitis, as well as reducing fever and relieving coughs. Herbal preparations of Abutilon hirtum typically involve making infusions, decoctions, or tinctures from the leaves, flowers, or roots, which can be used to treat various health issues.
